'''Connor Wynne''' (born 15 January 2001) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce|fb}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]].

He has spent time on loan from [[Hull FC]] at [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]] and the [[York City Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] .

==Background==
Wynne was born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], [[East Riding of Yorkshire]], England.

==Playing career==
===Hull F.C.===
In 2019, he made his [[Super League]] début for [[Hull FC]] against the [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP2921717|title=Super League: Salford Red Devils V Hull FC|publisher=BBC Sport|date=7 April 2019|access-date=10 November 2019}}</ref>
In round 11 of the [[2022 Super League season]], he scored a [[hat-trick]] in Hull F.C.'s 48-12 win over [[Toulouse Olympique]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/61245703/|title=Super League: Hull FC 48-12 Toulouse - Hat-tricks for Darnell McIntosh & Connor Wynne|work=BBC Sport|date=30 April 2022}}</ref> In May 2022, Wynne signed a contract extension until the end of the [[2023 Super League season|2023 season]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/new-contract-for-connor-wynne-who-brings-a-spark-to-hull-fc-3688602|title=New contract for Connor Wynne, who ‘brings a spark’ to Hull FC|work=Yorkshire Post|access-date=10 November 2024|date=10 May 2022|first=Peter|last=Smith}}</ref>

===Doncaster (loan)===
Wynne began the [[2019 RFL League 1|2019 season]] on [[RFL dual registration|dual-registration (DR) loan]] with [[RFL League 1]] club [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]], picking up an ankle injury in a friendly against [[Newcastle Thunder]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.doncasterfreepress.co.uk/news/doncaster-rlfc-v-hunslet-team-news-and-build-up-to-dons-opening-home-game-103353|title=Doncaster RLFC v Hunslet: Team news and build-up to Dons’ opening home game|work=Doncaster Free Press|access-date=10 November 2024|date=22 February 2019|first=Steven|last=Hossack}}</ref>

===York City (loan)===
On 21 April 2021, it was reported that he had signed for the [[York City Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a short-term loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=21 Apr 2021|accessdate=21 Apr 2021|title=York sign Connor Wynne on loan from Hull|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/york-sign-connor-wynne-on-loan-from-hull/}}</ref>

===Newcastle Thunder (loan)===
On 15 March 2023, Hull FC confirmed that Wynne had joined the club's dual-registration partner [[Newcastle Thunder]] on an indefinite loan.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/connor-wynne-joins-strong-hull-8248901|first=Daniel|last=Tomlinson|date=15 March 2023|access-date=10 November 2024|title=Connor Wynne joins strong Hull FC contingent at Newcastle Thunder as qualities explained by coach|work=Hull Daily Mail}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls (loan)===
On 25 April 2023, it was reported that he had signed for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a short-term loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Telegraph & Argus|date=25 Apr 2023|accessdate=25 Apr 2023|title=Bradford Bulls loan in young Hull FC back Connor Wynne|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/23478270.amp/}}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers===
On 6 November 2023 it was reported that he had signed for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=6 Nov 2023|title=Featherstone Rovers land released Hull FC starlet as rebuild continues at Post Office Road: 'An explosive competitor'|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/featherstone-rovers-land-released-hull-fc-starlet-as-rebuild-continues-at-post-office-road-an-explosive-competitor}}</ref> On 10 November 2024, Rovers confirmed that Wynne had signed a one-year contract extension for the 2025 season.<ref>{{cite web | website=Love Rugby League | access-date=10 November 2024 | date=10 November 2024 |title=Ex-Hull FC man secures future in Championship after ‘finding a home’ | first=Ben|last=Olawumi | url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ex-hull-fc-man-secures-future-in-championship-after-finding-a-home}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls===
On 8 November 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]] on a 1-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=8 Nov 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Former Hull FC star seals Super League return as length of contract confirmed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/connor-wynne-joins-bradford-ahead-of-2026}}</ref>
